When it may be in the best interest of the Government to consider award to other than the lowest priced offeror or other than the highest technically rated offeror.<SEP>When using a tradeoff process, the Government may consider factors other than cost or price.<SEP>The perceived benefits of the higher priced proposal shall merit the additional cost.<SEP>Allows the Government to accept other than the lowest priced proposal.

Tradeoff process
The solicitation shall state whether all evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are significantly more important than, approximately equal to, or significantly less important than cost or price.<SEP>All evaluation factors and significant subfactors that will affect contract award and their relative importance shall be clearly stated in the solicitation.

best value	Proposal
Source selection objective
The objective of source selection is to select the proposal that represents the best value.<SEP>The proposal that represents the best value is selected through the source selection objective.

Consideration,Evaluation Factor	Contracting Officer
Price or Cost
The contracting officer shall consider price or cost as one of the factors in the selection decision for each order placed under the contract.

Consideration,Factor	Non-Cost Evaluation Factors
Quality of Product or Service
The quality of the product or service shall be addressed in every source selection through consideration of one or more non-cost evaluation factors such as past performance, compliance with solicitation requirements, technical excellence, management capability, personnel qualifications, and prior experience (10 U.S.C. 3206(c)(1)(A) and 41 U.S.C. 3306(c)(1)(A)).

Consideration,Evaluation Factor	Past Performance
Source Selection
Past performance shall be evaluated in all source selections for negotiated competitive acquisitions expected to exceed the simplified acquisition threshold.

Solicitations
Past performance need not be evaluated if the contracting officer documents the reason past performance is not an appropriate evaluation factor for the acquisition.

data.tgt_id: Submission, modification, revision, and withdrawal of proposals
data.content:
Responsible parties	Offerors
Submission, modification, revision, and withdrawal of proposals
Offerors are responsible for submitting proposals, and any revisions, and modifications, so as to reach the Government office designated in the solicitation by the time specified in the solicitation.

Exception,Exclusion	Contracting Officer
Multiple-Award Contracts
The exception in paragraph (c)(1)(ii)(A) of this section shall not apply to solicitations for multiple-award contracts that provide for sole source orders pursuant to section 8(a) of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 637 (a)).

Consideration,Evaluation Factor	Past Performance
Solicitations
For solicitations that are not set aside for small business concerns, involving consolidation or bundling, the contracting officer shall include a factor to evaluate past performance indicating the extent to which the offeror attained applicable goals for small business participation under contracts that required subcontracting plans (15 U.S.C. 637(d)(4)(G)(ii)).

Authorizing,Deciding	Contracting officers
RFPs
Contracting officers may authorize receipt of proposals, modifications, or revisions by facsimile.<SEP>Contracting officers may issue RFPs.

Letter RFPs
Letter RFPs may be used in sole source acquisitions and other appropriate circumstances.

data.src_id: Exchanges with industry before receipt of proposals
data.content:
Contract award,Efficiency in proposal preparation,End users,Government acquisition,Government acquisition and supporting personnel,Government requirements,Industry capabilities,Information exchange,Negotiation,Procurement integrity,Proposal evaluation,Quality supplies and services,Reasonable prices	Exchanges with industry before receipt of proposals
Interests
Encouraged exchanges of information among all interested parties to improve understanding of Government requirements and industry capabilities, thereby allowing potential offerors to judge whether or how they can satisfy the Government’s requirements, and enhancing the Government’s ability to obtain quality supplies and services, including construction, at reasonable prices, and increase efficiency in proposal preparation, proposal evaluation, negotiation, and contract award.

Solicitations shall specify that award will be made on the basis of the lowest evaluated price of proposals meeting or exceeding the acceptability standards for non-cost factors.

Such communications shall not be used to cure proposal deficiencies or material omissions, materially alter the technical or cost elements of the proposal, and/or otherwise revise the proposal.<SEP>Such communications may be used to enhance Government understanding of proposals, allow reasonable interpretation of the proposal, or facilitate the Government’s evaluation process.<SEP>Such communications may be considered in rating proposals for the purpose of establishing the competitive range.

specific information
When specific information about a proposed acquisition is disclosed to potential offerors, it must be made available to the public as soon as practicable, but no later than the next general release of information, to avoid creating an unfair competitive advantage.

presolicitation or preproposal conference
When conducting a presolicitation or preproposal conference, materials distributed at the conference should be made available to all potential offerors, upon request.

specific information
When specific information about a proposed acquisition is disclosed to one or more potential offerors, that information must be made available to the public as soon as practicable, but no later than the next general release of information, in order to avoid creating an unfair competitive advantage.

Other	Proposals
Requests for Proposals
Requests for proposals solicit proposals from prospective contractors.

protected	confidential business strategy
potential offerors
Information provided to a potential offeror in response to its request must not be disclosed if doing so would reveal the potential offeror’s confidential business strategy, and is protected under 3.104 or subpart 24.2.

Other	Contracting Officers
Requests for Proposals
Contracting officers may issue Requests for Proposals and/or authorize receipt of proposals, modifications, or revisions by facsimile.

made available	potential offerors
presolicitation or preproposal conference
When conducting a presolicitation or preproposal conference, materials distributed at the conference should be made available to all potential offerors, upon request.

potential offeror

Requests for Proposals
Requests for proposals communicate Government requirements to prospective contractors.

data.src_id: American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
data.content:
specifies applicability	American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
bilateral contract modification
The contracting officer must specify the applicability of Alternate I to a bilateral contract modification that will use funds appropriated or otherwise made available by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.

Other	Requests for Proposals
Terms and Conditions
The solicitation may authorize offerors to propose alternative terms and conditions.

data.content:
specifies applicability	American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
task- or delivery-order contract
The contracting officer must specify the task or delivery orders to which Alternate I applies in a task- or delivery-order contract in which not all orders will use funds appropriated or otherwise made available by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.

cost-reimbursement contracts
The contracting officer shall use the clause with its Alternate II for cost-reimbursement contracts with State and local Governments, educational institutions, and other nonprofit organizations.

Assessment	Cost or price evaluation
Proposal evaluation
Proposal evaluation includes the cost or price evaluation as one of its components, which normally establishes price reasonableness and may include a cost analysis in limited situations to establish the reasonableness of the offeror's price.

<Organization,Individual>Include Clauses,Specify Clauses	Contracting Officer
Section I
The contracting officer is responsible for including the necessary clauses in Section I of the contract.

Indicator	Past performance evaluation
Proposal evaluation
Past performance information is considered one indicator of an offeror’s ability to perform the contract successfully, with the contracting officer documenting the evaluation.

Assessment,Comparison,Consideration,Documentation	Cost or price evaluation
Past performance evaluation
The Cost or Price Evaluation, Past Performance Evaluation is a comprehensive process conducted by the contracting officer to assess a contractor's past performance and its relevance to the current cost or price evaluation. This evaluation includes a cost realism analysis to determine what the Government should realistically expect to pay for the proposed effort. It is used to determine the offeror's understanding of the work and their ability to perform the contract successfully. The past performance evaluation is considered in the cost or price evaluation to establish the Government's expectations for the proposed effort's cost and to establish the reasonableness of the offeror's price. It is also used to assess the offeror's ability to perform the contract successfully. The past performance evaluation is a separate comparative assessment of past performance information, which is considered during the cost or price evaluation process. The contracting officer documents the cost or price evaluation, which includes the consideration of past performance information as one of its components. The past performance evaluation is also considered in the cost or price evaluation to establish the reasonableness of the otherwise successful offeror's price. The past performance evaluation is used in the cost or price evaluation to establish the Government's expectations for the proposed effort's cost. The past performance evaluation is used in the cost or price evaluation to establish the reasonableness of the offeror's price. The past performance evaluation is documented by the contracting officer as part of the cost or price evaluation process. The past performance evaluation is considered in the cost or price evaluation to determine the offeror's understanding of the work and their ability to perform the contract. The past performance evaluation is considered in the cost or price evaluation to determine the offeror's understanding of the work and their ability to perform the contract. The past performance evaluation is considered in the cost or price evaluation to determine the offeror's understanding of the work and their ability to perform the contract. The past performance evaluation is considered in the cost or price evaluation to determine the offeror's understanding of the work and their ability to perform the contract.
